data.title: As a developer, the ticket link command automatically promotes or rejects cross-boundary and redundant links
data.description:
## Description
**What**: Integrate resolve_hierarchy_link() into ticket-graph.py's add_dependency() function — the actual entry point for all ticket link commands (the shim routes ticket link to python3 ticket-graph.py --link, which calls add_dependency()). Before writing a LINK event, call resolve_hierarchy_link(). If is_redundant=True: exit non-zero with redundant with hierarchy error, write no LINK event. If was_redirected=True: print redirect notice to stderr AND emit machine-readable JSON to stdout, then proceed with the resolved pair.
**Why**: Wires the hierarchy resolver into the actual command path that users and agents run.
**Scope**:
- IN: Call resolve_hierarchy_link() within add_dependency(), redundant link rejection (SC7, SC8), redirect notice to stderr, machine-readable JSON to stdout on redirect for sub-agent observability, proceed with resolved pair
- OUT: Cycle detection integration (S3), unlink path hierarchy resolution (known limitation)

## Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, running ticket link task-A task-B depends_on where tasks are cross-story same-epic writes a story-to-story LINK event, prints redirect notice to stderr, and emits JSON {"redirected": true, "original": {...}, "resolved": {...}} to stdout
  ← Satisfies: SC3
- When this story is complete, running ticket link story-A epic-P depends_on where epic-P is story-A's direct parent exits non-zero with message containing redundant and writes no LINK event
  ← Satisfies: SC7
- When this story is complete, running ticket link task-A story-X depends_on where story-X is task-A's direct parent exits non-zero with redundant message and writes no LINK event
  ← Satisfies: SC8
- When this story is complete, the existing flock-based atomic write behavior in add_dependency() is preserved around the resolved pair
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations
- [Architecture] Integration point is add_dependency() in ticket-graph.py — NOT ticket-link.sh. Changes to ticket-link.sh will be dead code for link operations.
- [Scope] Promoted links (task-A→task-B promoted to story-X→story-Y) cannot be unlinked via original task IDs — ticket unlink task-A task-B will fail because no LINK event exists in task-A's directory. Document this behavioral change. Applying hierarchy resolution to the unlink path is out of scope for this epic.
- [Reliability] Existing flock-based atomic write in add_dependency() must still wrap the LINK event write for the resolved pair

## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
